These routes connected cities across Europe, North Africa, and the Middle East and were designed primarily to move armies, trade goods, and government messages efficiently across the empire.

Because Rome was the political and administrative center of this system, many of the major routes ultimately connected to the capital. Famous roads such as the Appian Way linked distant regions to Rome, which is why the saying became a metaphor meaning there are many different paths to reach the same destination.
